question_text: A container holds 200 g of water at 25°C. It is heated until 40 g of water evaporates. The remaining liquid is cooled and then frozen completely. Calculate the total mass of ice formed. (Assume no other substances are lost.)
explanation: Starting mass: 200 g. After evaporation: 200 g - 40 g = 160 g of water remains. Freezing is a physical change that only changes the state, not the mass. Therefore, 160 g of water becomes 160 g of ice. Evaporation and freezing are both physical changes, so the mass of the remaining substance is conserved.
